meaning: A culture where employees feel pressured to respond to messages at all hours.
definition: A culture where employees feel pressured to respond to messages at all hours.
example: To avoid always-on burnout, he turns off his work notifications at 6:00 PM.

meaning: A meeting where group members suggest a large number of ideas to solve a problem.
definition: A meeting where group members suggest a large number of ideas to solve a problem.
example: We held a brainstorming session on Mural to collect design ideas.

meaning: A central platform where documents, conversations, and tasks are integrated.
definition: A central platform where documents, conversations, and tasks are integrated.
example: Microsoft Teams acts as the collaboration hub for our entire department.

meaning: A designated block of hours during which all employees must be available.
definition: A designated block of hours during which all employees must be available.
example: Our core hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM for scheduling team meetings.

meaning: Sharing an office space with other self-employed people or remote workers.
definition: Sharing an office space with other self-employed people or remote workers.
example: She goes to a coworking hub twice a week to socialize and network.

meaning: Reserving a physical office desk in advance, usually via a mobile app.
definition: Reserving a physical office desk in advance, usually via a mobile app.
example: Use the desk booking app to secure a workspace before coming into the office.

meaning: Working together with others using digital platforms and online software.
definition: Working together with others using digital platforms and online software.
example: Digital collaboration tools have allowed us to design products with global partners.

meaning: A person who grew up in the digital age and is very familiar with technology.
definition: A person who grew up in the digital age and is very familiar with technology.
example: As a digital native, she easily learns how to use any new software app.

meaning: A measure of how active and involved employees are in company chat channels.
definition: A measure of how active and involved employees are in company chat channels.
example: The manager checks the engagement rate on team polls to see participation.

meaning: A system allowing employees to choose their start and finish work times.
definition: A system allowing employees to choose their start and finish work times.
example: Flex time allows him to drop his children at school before logging in.

meaning: A virtual connection or session between a mentor and a less experienced worker.
definition: A virtual connection or session between a mentor and a less experienced worker.
example: They established a mentoring link through bi-weekly video calls.

meaning: An organizational culture where working remotely is the primary option for all staff.
definition: An organizational culture where working remotely is the primary option for all staff.
example: Our company is remote-first, so all meetings are designed for online participants.

meaning: The amount of time spent looking at a digital screen like a computer or phone.
definition: The amount of time spent looking at a digital screen like a computer or phone.
example: I try to reduce my screen time in the evening by reading physical books.

meaning: An agreement or trust established during a remote interaction or video call.
definition: An agreement or trust established during a remote interaction or video call.
example: We closed the deal with a virtual handshake during our Zoom conference.

meaning: The practice of distributing tasks efficiently across team members.
definition: The practice of distributing tasks efficiently across team members.
example: The manager uses software for workload management to prevent employee burnout.
